mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness forces wage growth and signals robust aggregate demand, driving corporate earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in less than 50% of instances where UNRATE drops below 5.0, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls indicates synchronized consumer sector expansion, which historically precedes positive S&P 500 forward returns as earnings momentum accelerates.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a random baseline in out-of-sample testing, or forward returns are flat when both proxies rise simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects improving capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, directly supporting corporate profit margins and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production rises while S&P 500 forward returns remain negative for more than 60% of observations, indicating decoupled supply and equity performance.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2.0 percent signals rapid consumer demand contraction, which compresses near-term earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the signal shows no statistical significance in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous inflation above 2.0 percent and rising unemployment indicates stagflationary pressures, which force the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy while eroding real corporate margins, driving negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both inflation and unemployment rise concurrently, or the interaction term shows zero explanatory power in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth directly proxies aggregate corporate earnings momentum, mechanically translating higher economic expansion into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in more than 50% of quarters where real GDP growth exceeds the historical median, or the signal fails to predict returns in a walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During active monetary tightening cycles, rising federal funds rates combined with persistent inflation increase discount rates and compress equity valuations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances where both rates and inflation rise, or the joint signal shows no predictive edge over a buy-and-hold benchmark.
